title: Sicilian Defense
shortDescription: Black's sharpest reply to 1.e4, fighting for the center asymmetrically with 1...c5 and rich winning chances for both sides.

children.comment: The Sicilian Defense attacks d4 from the flank.
Black supports ...Nf6 and keeps several major Sicilian systems available.
White opens the center.
Black trades the c-pawn for White's central d-pawn.
Black develops with tempo against e4.
The Najdorf move prevents Nb5 and prepares ...b5.

line.name: Open Sicilian: Basic Setup
line.description: Black exchanges the c-pawn for White's d-pawn and gains an asymmetric central structure.

children.comment: Black gains space and attacks the d4 knight.

line.name: Najdorf: Classical Development
line.description: A simple Najdorf setup with ...e5, ...Be7 and kingside castling.

line.name: Najdorf: English Attack
line.description: Black meets Be3, f3 and Qd2 with a standard ...e5 and ...Be6 setup.

line.name: Najdorf: English Attack Main Line
line.description: Both sides castle on opposite wings and begin a direct attacking race.

children.comment: Black breaks the pin's tactical force and controls d5.

line.name: Najdorf: 6.Bg5
line.description: Black responds to the pin with ...e6 and prepares ...Be7 or ...b5.

children.comment: Black attacks b2 and enters the Poisoned Pawn Variation.

line.name: Najdorf: Poisoned Pawn Variation
line.description: Black accepts the b2 pawn and enters one of the sharpest opening battles in chess.

line.name: Najdorf: 6.h3
line.description: White prepares g4; Black reacts with ...e5 and controlled development.

children.comment: The Classical Sicilian develops naturally.

line.name: Classical Sicilian: Basic Setup
line.description: Black develops both knights quickly and keeps ...e6 or ...g6 flexible.

line.name: Classical Sicilian: Richter-Rauzer
line.description: Black meets Bg5 with ...e6 and develops against queenside castling.

children.comment: The Dragon begins with a kingside fianchetto.

line.name: Dragon Variation: Basic Setup
line.description: Black fianchettos the dark-squared bishop and pressures the long diagonal.

line.name: Dragon: Yugoslav Attack
line.description: Black develops the standard Dragon formation against long castling.

line.name: Dragon: Yugoslav Attack Main Line
line.description: Black enters the classic opposite-side castling battle.

children.comment: The Scheveningen structure controls d5 and f5.

line.name: Scheveningen: Basic Setup
line.description: Black builds the flexible small center with pawns on d6 and e6.

line.name: Scheveningen: Classical Main Line
line.description: Black uses the small center and waits for the correct pawn break.

children.comment: The Keres Attack begins immediate kingside expansion.

line.name: Scheveningen: Keres Attack
line.description: Black meets g4 with active queenside play and controlled kingside development.

children.comment: Black enters the Accelerated Dragon.

line.name: Accelerated Dragon: Basic Setup
line.description: Black skips ...d6 and uses ...d5 in one move when possible.

children.comment: Black achieves the thematic freeing break.

line.name: Accelerated Dragon: Central ...d5 Break
line.description: Black uses the key strategic advantage of playing ...d5 in one move.

children.comment: White establishes the Maroczy Bind.

line.name: Accelerated Dragon: Maroczy Bind
line.description: Black develops actively against White's c4 bind.

children.comment: The Sveshnikov gains space and attacks d4.

line.name: Sveshnikov: Main Setup
line.description: Black accepts a backward d6 pawn for active piece play and control of key squares.

line.name: Sveshnikov: Main Line
line.description: Black accepts structural weaknesses for dynamic piece activity.

children.comment: The Kalashnikov attacks d4 before developing the kingside knight.

line.name: Kalashnikov Variation
line.description: Black plays an early ...e5 without ...Nf6 and gains space immediately.

children.comment: White pins the knight and avoids main Open Sicilian theory.
Black accepts doubled pawns for the bishop pair and central control.

line.name: Rossolimo: Basic Response
line.description: Black answers Bb5 with ...g6 and prepares to recapture with the b-pawn.

line.name: Rossolimo: Main Line with ...g6
line.description: Black accepts doubled pawns and uses the bishop pair and central control.

children.comment: The Kan prevents Nb5 and keeps the structure flexible.

line.name: Kan Variation: Basic Setup
line.description: Black delays ...Nf6 and uses ...a6, ...Qc7 and ...b5 flexibly.

children.comment: White establishes a Maroczy-style bind.

line.name: Kan: Maroczy Structure
line.description: Black maneuvers against White's space advantage and prepares ...b5.

children.comment: The Taimanov develops naturally and eyes d4.

line.name: Taimanov Variation: Basic Setup
line.description: Black develops with ...Nc6 and ...Qc7 while keeping the center flexible.

line.name: Taimanov: English Attack Setup
line.description: Black meets Be3 and Qd2 with flexible development and queenside play.

children.comment: White avoids the Open Sicilian.

line.name: Closed Sicilian: Basic Response
line.description: Against Nc3 and g3, Black develops with ...Nc6, ...g6 and ...Bg7.

line.name: Closed Sicilian: Kingside Expansion
line.description: Black meets f4 with ...g6, ...Bg7 and central counterplay.

children.comment: White begins the Grand Prix Attack.

line.name: Grand Prix Attack: Basic Response
line.description: Black develops with ...Nc6, ...g6 and strikes in the center.

children.comment: White prepares d4 with a strong pawn center.
Black strikes in the center before White can consolidate.

line.name: Alapin Variation: Basic Response
line.description: Black immediately challenges White's d4 plan with ...d5.

line.name: Alapin: 2...d5 Main Line
line.description: Black exchanges in the center and attacks White's pawn structure.

line.name: Alapin: 2...Nf6
line.description: Black immediately attacks e4 and invites White to advance.

children.comment: White offers a pawn for development.
Black declines the pawn and attacks e4.

line.name: Smith-Morra Gambit: Decline with ...Nf6
line.description: Black declines the gambit and transposes toward an Alapin structure.

children.comment: Black accepts the gambit pawn.

line.name: Smith-Morra Gambit Accepted
line.description: Black takes the pawn and relies on accurate development to neutralize White's initiative.

children.comment: White offers a flank pawn to distract Black's c-pawn.
Black accepts the gambit.

line.name: Wing Gambit: Basic Response
line.description: Black accepts the b-pawn and challenges White's center.
